text: The importance of thyroid hormones (TH) in brain development has been recognised and investigated for many decades (Bernal, 2011; Williams 2008). Several human studies have shown that low levels of circulating maternal TH (as a consequence of a decrease of TH synthesis) can lead to neurophysiological deficits in the offspring, including learning and memory deficits, or even cretinism in most severe cases (Zoeller and Rovet, 2004; Henrichs et al., 2010).
